course_description.overview: Multi-day amateur stage race circling the island of Mallorca, featuring approximately 170 km total distance across stages on paved roads with typical Mallorcan terrain including coastal flats and inland climbs.

key_segments.name: Island loop stages
key_segments.description: Multi-stage format traversing varied Mallorcan landscapes from flat coastal sections to hilly interiors.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Challenging early-season test with moderate climbs and distance suited for amateur road racers seeking warm-weather preparation.

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Palma de Mallorca Airport (PMI); event based on island with good public transport and rentals.
logistics.accommodation: Hotels and rentals abundant across Mallorca, especially in Palma and coastal areas; book early for February peak.
logistics.registration_details: Amateur entry via official organizers; typically limited to Spanish residents or EU amateurs; check local cycling federation sites.
logistics.bike_logistics: Bike transport via airlines to PMI; local shops for service; fly-drive recommended.
logistics.local_considerations: Mild February weather (10-18°C); early-season traffic low; distinct from pro Challenge Mallorca and ultra Mallorca 312.

final_verdict.one_liner: Historic amateur stage race offering early-season Mallorca roads for dedicated road cyclists.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Amateur racers from Spain/EU seeking multi-day road racing in sunny February conditions.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes for warm-weather training and island racing experience at low cost.
pro_tips: Train for rolling terrain and short climbs typical of Mallorca.
Arrive early to acclimate and explore Serra de Tramuntana routes.
Combine with pro Challenge Mallorca spectator experience.
Secure bike box for flights; rent car for stage support.
